title: patch_block success-response envelope
description: Output contract for the patch_block typed MCP tool. Returned on success; failures route through UpstreamError per Principle IV and are documented in errors.md. Authoritative source is the Zod schema in src/tools/patch_block/schema.ts. Strict — additional properties forbidden.

path.description: Vault-relative path of the note that was patched. Echoed for write-verification. For target_mode='specific', identical to the input's file/path. For target_mode='active', the focused-file path returned by the pre-write eval.

vault.description: Vault display name. Echoed for write-verification. For target_mode='specific', identical to the input's vault. For target_mode='active', the display name corresponding to the focused-file's vault root.

block_id.description: The block-id locator from the input, echoed verbatim (bare identifier, no leading caret). Caller cross-references against the request to confirm the intended target.

block_shape.description: Which surgery mechanic was applied by the wrapper. 'paragraph' = marker was the trailing token on a paragraph's final line; the body was replaced and the trailing ' ^block-id' was preserved. 'list-item' = marker was the trailing token on a list-item line; the list-marker bytes + indentation were preserved, the item content was replaced, and the trailing ' ^block-id' was preserved. 'separately-placed' = marker was a standalone line immediately following a table / callout / blockquote / indented-code block; the marker line was preserved verbatim and only the preceding block's content was replaced. The on-heading shapes (on-heading-atx / on-heading-setext) never appear in the success envelope because they short-circuit to BLOCK_ON_HEADING per FR-019a.

bytes_written.description: Total bytes written to disk (post-edit file size in UTF-8 bytes). Provides a coarse confirmation signal — a near-zero value when the caller intended a substantial write is a red flag worth surfacing in audit logs.
